basics.name: Robert DeLanghe
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer
basics.headline: AI agents ship code. I write the contracts that keep them in check.
basics.summary: Programmer building the governance layer for agentic systems, baked into the pipeline. Writes the contracts that make agent-authored code safe to ship: declarative, capability-scoped, enforced before deploy. Open-sourced as prx; gating production deploys at Aura.

work.name: Bounded Systems
work.summary: Capability security for AI agents: software delivered by agents, governed like infrastructure. Generalized from my Aura work into open tooling.
highlights: Shared the 58 capability-security tools behind my own production work — one public contract, each a single sanctioned access point that bounds an agent to what it holds.

work.name: Aura
work.summary: On Commerce, the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems. I ship the integration and automation backbone, and built the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing.
highlights: Wrote the orchestration CLI that replaced Aura's 9-tab spreadsheet supply plan with one source of truth, compiling into dozens of downstream schema contracts checked by hundreds of test scenarios.

Investigated unreviewed agent-authored commits reaching main: reconstructed each one's entry path across five log domains, authored the post-mortem, and designed the controls that became Aura's policy for shipping AI-written code.

work.name: L2L
work.summary: Owned the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team, spanning static-analysis CI, a design system, and frontend performance.
highlights: Shipped static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline, blocking whole defect classes at merge instead of catching them after. Critical bugs fell 20% across the team.

work.name: Pioneer Works
work.summary: Delivered the event-planning platform for a major Brooklyn arts center.
highlights: Derived event status from attached documents, not a human-set flag, across the venue's large concerts.

work.name: Kaleida
work.summary: Automated just-in-time production for an AR and print-making studio.
highlights: Eliminated edition-numbering errors with a database that issued serial edition IDs on request, making a duplicate impossible by construction.

work.name: Scope of Work
work.summary: Engineered the contact-management system for the manufacturing publication.
highlights: Designed the schema that moved spreadsheet data into a relational database, so a malformed record couldn't be written.

work.name: Recurse Center
work.summary: Type theory in Haskell, using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. The intellectual root of the capability work: push correctness into the boundary so violations can't compile.
highlights: Completed a selective 12-week programming retreat researching Haskell type theory and how a well-constrained type narrows the space of possible implementations.

projects.name: prx
projects.description: Agent-run pipeline that drives each work unit to a merged PR through one signed, content-addressed flow, with git-writes verified against the capability owner that holds them. Evolved from hooksmith; moved to Zod/TypeScript when the load-bearing contracts proved to be between systems, not machine and kernel.

projects.name: ocap-provenance
projects.description: SLSA/in-toto provenance extended with object-capability semantics: each attestation records not just who produced an artifact but which capabilities the producer held and which were denied.

meta._source: Canonical résumé doc in JSON Resume format (jsonresume.org). Authored + signed off here (review.mjs) and published to bdelanghe/site, where build.mjs renders /resume + /resume.json. Curated from a saved LinkedIn webarchive, NOT live-fetched. Site render-context (banner, intro, seeking, nav links, the decorative place line) lives in presentation.json. Edit here, not in site.
